title: Add pod, volume, network to inspect package
body:
podman inspect only had the capabilities to inspect containers and images. if a user wanted to inspect a pod, volume, or network, they would have to use `podman network inspect`, `podman pod inspect` etc. Docker's cli allowed users to inspect both volumes and networks using regular inspect, so this commit gives the user the functionality

If the inspect type is not specified using --type, the order of inspection is:

containers
images
volumes
networks
pods

meaning if container that has the same name as an image, podman inspect would return the container inspect.

To avoid duplicate code, podman network inspect and podman volume inspect now use the inspect package as well. Podman pod inspect does not because podman pod inspect returns a single json object while podman inspect can return multiple)

Closes #6757 

Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>

comments.body: I would put pods at the bottom of the inspect order - it would be closer to Docker's current behavior, since they don't have pods.

Any ideas on how to fix the vendoring and validation tests? `sudo make vendor-in-container` is  failing for me with a 
```
Error: error configuring network namespace for container 3dbda7f4b543296edc30e24ed5c9f4f09f64f9ec3d443f2dd402fcdf2b4f9e84: No CNI configuration file in /etc/cni/net.d/. Has your network provider started?
```
Could you run `make vendor-in-container` and see if it changes anything?

@rhatdan throws a 
```
github.com/containers/podman/v2/cmd/podman/varlink: open /src/cmd/podman/varlink/iopodman.go: permission denied
github.com/containers/podman/v2/pkg/varlink: open /src/pkg/varlink/iopodman.go: permission denied
make: *** [Makefile:677: vendor] Error 1
make: *** [Makefile:683: vendor-in-container] Error 2
```

but it looks like it did something so i re-pushed
